case_title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE VS. JAVIER BARAJAS PEREZ ET AL

docket_entries.description: LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO FEB-19-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND OF NONAPPEARANCE; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MATTER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7(B). JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

PROOF OF SERVICE OF DECLARATION OF TRISTAN P. ESPINOZA, ESQ. , REGARDING ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE; PROPOSED ORDER; AND NOTICE OF REMOTE APPEARANCE (TRANSACTION ID # 75700335) FILED BY PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MARCH-19-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED AND OF NONAPPEARANCE; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE'S MOTION TO DEEM ADMITTED THE TRUTH OF ANY MATTERS SPECIFIED IN ITS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE, SERVED ON DEFENDANT PACIFIC STRUCTURES, INC., IS GRANTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE's Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, served on defendant Pacific Structures, Inc., is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by defendant Pacific Structures Inc. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $460 payable by defendant to plaintiff. Defendant shall remit sanctions within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
